briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for July 15, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis: Extreme Phenomena and Policy Challenges at Home and Abroad
sections.content: Today's top news clearly demonstrates the deepening climate crisis worldwide. Record-breaking heatwaves in Colorado are adding to 'compound extreme events' [138], while in Europe, heatwave-related deaths are surging, and a deadly wildfire in Spain has claimed the lives of 12 foreign nationals, among other casualties [145, 154, 156]. A report from the UK indicates that the country faces 'unprecedented' climate change, with extreme temperatures becoming entrenched, and an analysis suggesting that most heatwave reports ignored climate change serves as a stark warning [151, 155, 148]. Domestically, concerns have been raised that green spaces created for climate change adaptation are, paradoxically, exacerbating gentrification [2, 24]. Changes in the pittas and marine biodiversity are acting as harbingers of ecological disruption [11, 8, 26]. Furthermore, a warning that sourcing power for AI and semiconductor mega-projects from fossil fuels would make achieving the 2035 greenhouse gas target impossible and could delay the achievement of the Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) by up to 14 years, points to significant policy challenges [44, 110, 135]. The failure of discussions to amend the Carbon Neutrality Act in the Special Committee on Climate Change and criticisms that fuel tax reduction policies hinder carbon neutrality also highlight difficulties in policy implementation [37, 38, 43].

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Change Adaptation Efforts: The Role of Smart Technology and Local Communities
sections.content: Amidst this crisis, domestic and international efforts for climate change adaptation are unfolding in various ways. The Rural Development Administration is expanding the distribution of ginseng varieties resilient to climate change and conducting on-site inspections [1], while Muju County has completed the distribution of multi-purpose sunshade nets to prevent damage from heatwaves and hail [5, 18, 19, 25, 27]. Smart agricultural technologies are being actively utilized in response to climate change, with Hwaseong Special City using drones to control rice pests and diseases, and Imsil County focusing administrative efforts on preventing the spread of fruit tree anthracnose [6, 15, 16, 22, 14, 21]. At the urban level, Mapo-gu is expanding cooling centers [30], and Goyang City is broadening its heatwave safety net by automatically enrolling all citizens in 'Gyeonggi Climate Insurance,' which guarantees compensation for heatwave damage [32]. Seongbuk-gu operates 'water refill stations' with the dual goals of heatwave countermeasures and carbon neutrality practices [61, 81, 85, 88], and Geumcheon-gu Facilities Management Corporation has completed practical simulation training for climate change and novel disaster preparedness [31]. Furthermore, adaptation capabilities to climate change are being strengthened in the health and infrastructure sectors, including efforts to expand 'quarantine-safe zones' for proactive prevention of climate change-mediated infectious diseases [13] and KHNP's climate-response design inspection for Shin Hanul Units 3 & 4 [20]. KCL (Korea Conformity Laboratories) is seeking solutions to the climate crisis through technology that can instantly simulate climate crisis phenomena such as heatwaves, heavy rains, and heavy snow [34].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Transition to a Carbon-Neutral Society: Renewable Energy Expansion and New Markets
sections.content: Efforts to transition to a carbon-neutral society are actively underway across the country, with notable progress, particularly in the renewable energy sector. The Ministry of Climate has pilot-introduced a PPA (Power Purchase Agreement) brokerage platform, allowing direct online buying and selling of renewable energy, with official operation scheduled to begin in August [91, 93, 95, 96, 98, 99, 100, 101, 102, 105, 106, 115, 120, 122, 124, 125, 131, 133]. This is expected to significantly contribute to activating renewable energy transactions by directly connecting RE100 companies with power generators [92, 94]. Globally, renewable energy generation recorded its largest-ever increase, accounting for 31.7% of the world's electricity production [103, 104]. Domestically, KEPCO KDN is leveraging AI and ESS to resolve challenges in connecting renewable energy to the grid and is fully committed to stabilizing the power network [107, 109, 111, 121]. Furthermore, LG Energy Solution is supplying up to 2.9 GWh of batteries for Google's solar and ESS projects [123, 127], and KHNP is advancing its renewable energy-linked water electrolysis technology, contributing to hydrogen production facilities protecting the power grid [116]. Carbon neutrality practices are also spreading within local communities. Pyeongtaek City is establishing an 'Environmental Education Five-Year Plan' to leap forward as a leading carbon-neutral city [50, 60], and Jangseong County is providing incentives, such as local currency, to residents participating in carbon neutrality practices [64, 76]. The Korea SMEs and Startups Agency (KOSME) is collaborating with large corporations to support SMEs' carbon-neutral facility investments and holding discussions for carbon neutrality within supply chains, thereby assisting the industry's transition to carbon neutrality [36, 47, 56, 74, 83].

sections.title: 🌍 Global Cooperation and Awareness Raising: The Importance of Climate Action
sections.content: Responding to climate change has now become an essential task for global cooperation, transcending national borders. Ambassador Kyun Jong-ho for Climate Change emphasized solidarity with the international community through the message 'Crisis is Opportunity' [3, 17]. Daegu Suseong-gu is expanding inter-city cooperation and international exchange by hosting a climate change response forum with Karlsruhe, Germany [4, 7, 10, 28]. As the UN Climate Change Conference (COP31) is scheduled to be held in Antalya in 2026 [140], technological cooperation is also active, with HLRS developing digital twin tools for environmental and climate change management [142]. International collaboration to address climate change is strengthening in various sectors, such as South Korea and China opening carbon-neutral shipping routes and accelerating cooperation on eco-friendly vessels [58]. Domestically, the 'Citizen Environmental College' with Seoul National University faculty is training carbon-neutral green leaders [52, 65, 73, 79], and the Chungnam Climate and Environment Education Center is expanding experiential education for carbon neutrality by establishing a school environmental education cooperation network [77]. These educational activities continue to raise public awareness and promote action on climate change. These efforts once again remind us of the importance of climate action for a sustainable future.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, alongside various domestic and international efforts and technological advancements to overcome it. Only sustained attention and action can protect our future. This concludes the climate briefing for July 15, 2026.
